axis_responsibility.Agent: validate_transition_concern_evidence enforces the emission invariant as a SHAPE rule (which concern_kind may carry a reroute-targeting address) and returns binding:false evidence only; never judges success/quality and chooses no Movement.
axis_responsibility.Brick: Declares concern shape and which refs a concern_kind may carry (transition-concern-return.yaml + QA return.yaml + brick.md prose).
axis_responsibility.Link: Gate/route policy adopts or pauses a reroute-eligible concern; owns Movement and target disposition. Unchanged.
axis_responsibility.Support: walker_transition_concern.py resolves named targets and records HOLD/walk-on; checkers emit support evidence only; authors no route, Movement, success, or quality.

candidate_file_changes: agent/return_fact.py (PROPOSED only; within declared write_scope agent/return_fact.py + agent/**/*)
AGENTS.md (PROPOSED-CONDITIONAL; include only if AGENTS.md is in the declared write_scope and code<->doc parity requires it -- not confirmed in the inspected write_scope lines)

checker_or_verifier_plan.anti_tautology: The negative case must establish that the PRE-fix path WOULD have rerouted (verification_gap + brick target resolves single via the existing classifier) so the new invariant demonstrably bites rather than passing vacuously.
checker_or_verifier_plan.command_surface: Validation rides the existing check_profile.py --all framework; core profiles already include bounded_agent_proposed_routing_loop and building_operator_driver0. No new runtime infrastructure.
checker_or_verifier_plan.negative_new_behavioral_optional: Companion driver-level case: a code-attack-qa brick emitting verification_gap with a brick target produces NO recorded second work attempt (frontier HOLD), mirroring the positive case shape.
checker_or_verifier_plan.negative_new_unit: Add a unit case (check_bounded_agent_proposed_routing_loop0.py or kernel_checks.py) driving validate_transition_concern_evidence: verification_gap + declared non-self brick: target -> ValueError/invalid -> HOLD (no adopted reroute); verification_gap + building-boundary: sentinel/empty -> accepted, walks on.
checker_or_verifier_plan.parity_and_drift: Run kernel_checks.py concern_kind parity (extend only if the split is encoded as mirrored data) and check_bricks_spec_completeness prose-drift guard after any brick.md/return.yaml edit.
checker_or_verifier_plan.positive_keep_green: building_operator_driver0.yaml driver0-live-qa-concern-reroutes-to-work (implementation_gap -> adopt -> second work attempt) must stay green, proving a real defect still reroutes.

returned.design_summary: Root cause of the partial patch: the QA concern-emission rule lives only as PROSE in the two QA brick.md bodies; no machine artifact prevents a non-defect (verification_gap / environment) concern from naming a brick: target and thereby being adopted by the existing single-target classifier as an auto-reroute. The proposed minimal root fix encodes the policy as an Agent-axis SHAPE invariant: partition the existing closed TRANSITION_CONCERN_KINDS into REROUTE_ELIGIBLE_CONCERN_KINDS (implementation_gap, design_gap, upstream_gap, boundary_mismatch, insufficient_input, replay_needed) and a NON-reroute kind (verification_gap), then have validate_transition_concern_evidence reject a verification_gap concern that carries a non-self brick-targeting related_boundary_ref (one that would auto-reroute). Benign verification_gap evidence stays expressible via empty related_boundary_refs or a building-boundary: sentinel, which the walker already WALKS ON. This is a pure partition of a closed vocab (no new concern_kind, no new field, no new Movement literal, no new fact class, no scheduler, no provider runtime, no success/quality judge). The walker, Link adoption policy, and binding:false rule are unchanged. Coverage is locked by adding NEGATIVE checker evidence mirroring the existing positive driver case, and the prose in both QA brick.md bodies is tightened to name verification_gap explicitly so the human-facing rule matches the machine rule (drift-guarded). Truth-before-quality is preserved: the validator enforces shape, support/checkers remain support evidence only, and QA still returns no success/failure/approval and chooses no Movement.
edge_cases: verification_gap with empty related_boundary_refs -> accepted (pure verification-gap/not_proven record).
verification_gap with a building-boundary: sentinel -> accepted; walker walks on (no reroute).
verification_gap naming a non-self declared brick -> rejected by I1 -> invalid-concern HOLD for a human, not a silent reroute.

verification_gap naming the SOURCE brick (self-ref) -> walker already strips self to non_reroute; OPEN whether I1 should also flag self-ref or treat self/sentinel uniformly as the non-reroute channel.
Reroute-eligible kind carrying only a building-boundary: sentinel -> already walk-on (concern raised, no address proposed); preserved.
unknown concern_kind -> OPEN: leave permissive under general shape rules, or fold into the non-reroute side; current proposal keeps it under general shape rules unless I6 totality forces a choice.
Mixed valid brick ref + garbage ref on a reroute-eligible kind -> unchanged: walker returns none/unresolvable_reroute_address HOLD (already covered).
verification_gap with a brick: target that does NOT resolve to a declared node -> already HOLDs via walker none path; I1 would additionally reject at validation time (earlier surface).
invariants: I1: A verification_gap concern may not carry a non-self brick-targeting related_boundary_ref (only building-boundary: sentinel or empty allowed) -> it can never auto-request reroute; a brick-targeting verification_gap is rejected to a human-visible invalid-concern HOLD rather than silently dropped or silently rerouted.

I2: REROUTE_ELIGIBLE_CONCERN_KINDS (implementation_gap, design_gap, upstream_gap, boundary_mismatch, insufficient_input, replay_needed) MAY name an upstream WORK brick that Link MAY adopt under declared policy (existing behavior preserved).
I3: binding stays false; no concern carries movement/target/target_ref/route_target/success/quality (already in forbidden_transition_concern_fields).
I4: concern_kind vocabulary code<->doc parity stays green; any encoded eligibility split is mirrored or left out of the parity check.
I5: support and checkers remain support evidence only; a failing/missing probe stays an observed fact, not a verdict (truth-before-quality preserved).

I6: REROUTE_ELIGIBLE_CONCERN_KINDS union {verification_gap} (and unknown, per open question) equals TRANSITION_CONCERN_KINDS -- the partition stays total over the closed vocab so no kind is silently unclassified.
not_proven: Implementation readiness -- no code was written or executed; these are proposed targets only (this is a read-only design Brick; write_effective is false).
That the proposed invariant leaves all currently-green checkers green -- check_profile.py --all was NOT run here.
Whether the Agent-contract validator vs a Link route-policy declaration is the semantically correct seat for the eligibility rule -- structure observed, runtime behavior not executed.
Whether the chosen REROUTE_ELIGIBLE_CONCERN_KINDS partition matches every real QA scenario (semantic fitness of the split).
Whether self-ref and unknown concern_kind should be constrained by I1 or left to the existing walk-on/permissive paths (open design questions).

Whether AGENTS.md is inside the declared write_scope -- the inspected write_scope lines showed agent/**, brick/templates/bricks/**, support/operator/**, support/checkers/** but an AGENTS.md glob was not confirmed (one allowed_paths line was elided).
That the building-boundary: sentinel walk-on is the correct universal 'no reroute' channel across every walker path, not just the classifier branches inspected.
Real-provider QA emission behavior -- whether agents in practice select verification_gap for environment cases.
Source truth, success, quality, and Movement sufficiency -- explicitly out of this Brick's reach (owned by Link gate and the human).
observed_evidence: agent/return_fact.py:9-19 TRANSITION_CONCERN_KINDS is a closed frozenset {design_gap, implementation_gap, upstream_gap, boundary_mismatch, insufficient_input, replay_needed, verification_gap, unknown}; verification_gap confirmed at line 17.

agent/return_fact.py:112-134 validate_transition_concern_evidence enforces SHAPE only: allowed keys, concern_ref must start 'transition-concern:', concern_kind in TRANSITION_CONCERN_KINDS, binding must be False, reason_refs non-empty, related_boundary_refs prefixes limited to brick:/brick-/brick-boundary:/brick-instance:/building-boundary:. NO rule couples concern_kind to whether a reroute-targeting (brick:) address may be carried.

support/operator/walker_transition_concern.py:30-194 _classify_reroute_target resolves named related_boundary_refs against EXISTING declared Brick nodes and classifies single (reroute), ambiguous (HOLD), non_reroute (building-boundary: sentinel OR source-only self-ref -> WALK ON, no reroute), none (HOLD); an unresolvable brick-targeting ref co-occurring with a valid one -> none with hold_reason unresolvable_reroute_address. Classification keys on the REFS, never on concern_kind. The 'raise concern without reroute' channel already exists via the building-boundary: sentinel and empty/self refs.

brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md:58-62 carries the emission policy as PROSE ONLY: real defect -> aim related_boundary_refs at the upstream WORK node; 'Environment or runtime constraints (no temp dir, write-scope limits, provider limits, read-only status, "live not run") are NOT defects -- record them in not_proven, never as a transition_concern.'
brick/templates/bricks/axis-attack-qa/brick.md:54-59 carries the identical prose policy for the axis-attack QA kind.

brick/templates/bricks/transition-concern-return.yaml:5-33 declares the shared non-binding concern shape (concern_kind enum incl verification_gap; binding:false; related_boundary_refs names the upstream WORK node; forbidden movement/target/route_target/success/quality). concern_kind is enumerated but NOT partitioned by reroute-eligibility.

brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/return.yaml:7-48 and axis-attack-qa/return.yaml:7-48 list transition_concern_evidence + not_proven as required_return_shape members and forbid success/failure/approved/quality/movement_choice/route_target.

support/checkers/profiles/building_operator_driver0.yaml:144-145 case driver0-live-qa-concern-reroutes-to-work (case_kind live_qa_reroute_to_work_n2) is the POSITIVE proof: a valid implementation_gap concern targeting the upstream work brick is adopted and a second work attempt is recorded (the partial 88cd2cc proof). No NEGATIVE case proves a verification_gap/environment concern does NOT auto-request reroute.

support/checkers/check_bounded_agent_proposed_routing_loop0.py already constructs a verification_gap concern callable (per prior design evidence ~677-704) used to exercise classifier target seams, not an emission-policy invariant.

support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py:547-588 _axis_vocab_check_concern_kind_parity locks code<->doc parity of the concern_kind vocabulary between agent/return_fact.py and AGENTS.md; no checker partitions the vocab into reroute-eligible vs non-reroute.

support/checkers/check_bricks_spec_completeness.py:314-642 the prose-return-shape drift guard iterates the SECONDARY/concern return template so transition_concern_evidence prose in QA brick.md cannot drift from the return.yaml; any brick.md/return.yaml edit is re-validated.

project/brick-protocol/buildings/qa-concern-emission-policy-fix-0625-v2/declared-building-plan.json:48-55 (and the repeated per-step write_scope blocks) declares globbed allowed_paths: agent/return_fact.py, brick/templates/bricks/**/*, support/operator/**/*, support/checkers/**/*, agent/**/* (v2 corrects the v1 bare-directory write_scope error).

Prior v1 design (project/brick-protocol/buildings/qa-concern-emission-policy-fix-0625/work/step-outputs/.../design-attempt-1/step-output.json:123-266) reached the design/COO gate with the same root-fix shape (REROUTE_ELIGIBLE partition + negative checker); this v2 re-runs design under corrected globbed write scopes.
returned.output_excerpt: I have independently confirmed all anchors. Here is the design contract. ```json { "observed_evidence": [ "agent/return_fact.py:9-19 TRANSITION_CONCERN_KINDS is a closed frozenset {design_gap, implementation_gap, upstream_gap, boundary_mismatch, insufficient_input, replay_needed, verification_gap, unknown}; verification_gap confirmed at line 17.", "agent/return_fact.py:112-134 validate_transition_concern_evidence enforces SHAPE only: allowed keys, concern_ref must start 'transition-concern:', concern_kind in TRANSITION_CONCERN_KINDS, binding must be False, reason_refs non-empty, related_bou...
proposed_changes: agent/return_fact.py (PROPOSED): add REROUTE_ELIGIBLE_CONCERN_KINDS as a named subset of the existing closed TRANSITION_CONCERN_KINDS (verification_gap excluded). In validate_transition_concern_evidence, after the existing shape checks, when concern_kind is NOT reroute-eligible AND any related_boundary_ref is a brick-targeting ref (brick:/brick-/brick-boundary:/brick-instance:) that is not the source/self node, raise ValueError so support surfaces an invalid-concern HOLD instead of an auto-reroute. building-boundary: sentinels and empty related_boundary_refs remain accepted. No new vocabulary value; pure partition of the closed set.

brick/templates/bricks/transition-concern-return.yaml (PROPOSED): document the concern_kind<->reroute-eligibility split in shape comments (no new field); state that verification_gap is the non-reroute / verification-gap bucket and must use building-boundary: sentinel or empty related_boundary_refs.

brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md (PROPOSED): tighten the existing prose (lines ~58-62) to name verification_gap explicitly as the environment/verification bucket recorded in not_proven (or as a non-reroute verification_gap concern), keeping the body GENERAL to the kind so the prose-drift guard re-validates.
brick/templates/bricks/axis-attack-qa/brick.md (PROPOSED): same prose tightening for parity with code-attack-qa.

support/checkers/check_bounded_agent_proposed_routing_loop0.py (and/or support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py) (PROPOSED): add NEGATIVE unit coverage driving validate_transition_concern_evidence: verification_gap + a declared non-self brick: target -> ValueError/invalid -> HOLD (no adopted reroute); verification_gap + building-boundary: sentinel (or empty) -> accepted, walker walks on. Anti-tautology: assert the PRE-fix path would have rerouted so the invariant demonstrably bites.

support/checkers/check_building_operator_driver0.py + support/checkers/profiles/building_operator_driver0.yaml (PROPOSED-OPTIONAL): companion behavioral negative case mirroring live_qa_reroute_to_work_n2 -- a verification_gap concern naming a brick target produces NO recorded second work attempt (frontier HOLD).

support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py concern_kind parity (PROPOSED-CONDITIONAL): extend _axis_vocab_check_concern_kind_parity to also lock the eligibility partition ONLY if the split is encoded as mirrored data the doc must restate; otherwise leave the vocab parity untouched.

AGENTS.md (PROPOSED-CONDITIONAL, only if within declared write_scope and required for code<->doc parity): annotate the concern_kind vocab block with the reroute-eligibility split; keep consistent so parity stays green.

relevant_current_structure.agent_axis_contract: agent/return_fact.py:9-19 TRANSITION_CONCERN_KINDS (closed vocab) + 112-134 validate_transition_concern_evidence (canonical SHAPE gate consumed by support). This is the proposed seat of the eligibility invariant.
relevant_current_structure.brick_axis_templates: brick/templates/bricks/transition-concern-return.yaml (shared concern shape) and code-attack-qa/axis-attack-qa return.yaml + brick.md (prose policy, drift-guarded by check_bricks_spec_completeness).
relevant_current_structure.existing_coverage: Positive: building_operator_driver0.yaml:144-145. Parity: kernel_checks.py:547 _axis_vocab_check_concern_kind_parity. Classifier seams: check_bounded_agent_proposed_routing_loop0.py. Prose drift: check_bricks_spec_completeness.py.
relevant_current_structure.link_adoption: Default-transition / declared COO-Link policy adopts a reroute-eligible concern (proven by building_operator_driver0.yaml live_qa_reroute_to_work_n2). Owns Movement and target disposition; proposed unchanged.
relevant_current_structure.support_mechanics: support/operator/walker_transition_concern.py classifies named refs into single/ambiguous/non_reroute/none; building-boundary: sentinel and source-only self-ref already WALK ON (no reroute); invalid concern -> HOLD. Classification keys on REFS, not concern_kind.

returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No new Movement literal; Link still owns adoption, Movement, and target disposition.
No new fact class, scheduler, provider runtime, success judge, or quality judge.
AgentFact stays closed (received_work, returned); transition_concern_evidence stays nested non-binding evidence with binding:false.
Walker classifier branches single/ambiguous/non_reroute/none keep their mechanics; the building-boundary: sentinel / self-ref walk-on channel is reused, not changed.
Existing positive reroute path (implementation_gap adoption -> rework, building_operator_driver0.yaml live_qa_reroute_to_work_n2) preserved and must stay green.
concern_ref / reason_refs / related_boundary_refs prefix rules and forbidden_transition_concern_fields unchanged.
